summary: Set 033 Session 2 of 6: shipped the TypeScript reader-side check-out / check-in migration (H2). MarkerWatchService.ts renamed to inProgressSetsService.ts (git mv preserved blame) and reduced to two surviving exports — listInProgressSets(all?: SessionSet[]) which filters in-progress sets and sorts by startedAt asc (oldest in-flight ranks first), and the unchanged extractRecommendation() parser. The class, marker-file watcher, walk-up resolver, polling backstop, slug-integrity check, and multi-in-progress ambiguity-banner machinery were retired wholesale since the workspace-level file-watcher already in extension.ts watching **/session-state.json covers every signal the new reader needs. types.ts OrchestratorInfo extended with the S1 fields (provider, checkedOutAt, lastActivityAt) and fileSystem.ts parses them through to LiveSession.orchestrator. New OrchestratorAccordion helper accordionStateFromOrchestratorBlock(block, recommendation) synthesizes the unchanged RenderState/OrchestratorMarker shape from the simpler check-out record — signalKind='current' + confidence='high' (writer is authoritative); tier via classifyRecommendationTier(); thinking=false; updatedAt falls back through lastActivityAt → checkedOutAt → now. CustomSessionSetsView refactored so every in-progress row gets its own accordion (multi-in-progress is the supported case); banner removed from the protocol + host snapshot builder + webview client.js end-to-end. Suppression key field renamed across the wire: markerUpdatedAt → accordionUpdatedAt (sourced from orchestrator.lastActivityAt, preserving the naturally-aging suppression semantic). scripts/write-orchestrator-marker.js (613 lines) and docs/orchestrator-marker-schema.md deleted; the one stale .dabbler/orchestrator.json marker under docs/session-sets/029-orchestrator-model-effort-gauges/.dabbler/ purged. extension.ts dropped the MarkerWatchService import + construction. Two in-session decisions surfaced via AskUserQuestion per the spec's flagged choice points: (1) operator chose to rename the file to inProgressSetsService.ts for clarity; (2) operator chose the spec-default delete of orchestrator-marker-schema.md. Spec risk R1 acknowledged: between S2 ship and S3 ship the Claude SessionStart hook + Codex configWatcher + setOrchestratorManual reference the deleted script via HELPER_REL string-path — compile-time clean, runtime fails until S3's H1 hook-becomes-invoker refactor lands. Layer-2 tests rewritten: 7 preserved extractRecommendation tests + 3 new listInProgressSets tests (filter+sort, empty result, null-startedAt tolerance) — full Mocha-stub suite 401 pass + 2 pre-existing unrelated failures (configEditor-foundation + notificationsSection). Layer-3 Playwright spec rewritten: the two retired-signalKind marker scenarios removed, replaced with (1) orchestrator-block-fed provider-sublabel scenario and (2) multi-in-progress-rendering scenario that asserts both rows expandable AND .ambiguity-banner count 0. `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Round A via gemini-pro $0.051 VERIFIED with no must-fix issues — all 7 assertion groups (H2 retirement, synthesizer correctness, suppression continuity, banner removal, Layer-2/3 coverage, edge cases) cross-referenced to named functions/cited code. Session 2 closes near top of $0.10-$0.25 forecast band (one round at $0.051); ~$0.09 of $1.25 total Set 033 routed-call NTE consumed across S1+S2.

reason.specifics: Session 3 is the UI affordance migration: rename dabbler.setOrchestrator → dabbler.checkOutOrchestrator ('Check Out As…'), add the dabbler.releaseCheckOut Command Palette action (one of H3's two named release paths), refactor the Claude SessionStart hook + Codex configWatcher + Gemini/Copilot installer shims to invoke `python -m ai_router.start_session` rather than write the marker directly (H1: hooks become invokers). This closes the writer-side breakage S2 left in place (spec risk R1) — between S2 ship and S3 ship, the three S3-scope command files reference the deleted scripts/write-orchestrator-marker.js via HELPER_REL string-path. Same model + effort holds: high-effort is right because the spec lists six call sites to refactor (package.json contributes.commands rename + module rename + ActionRegistry copy update + Command Palette release action + four hook installer touchpoints) and the cross-file rename surface is large enough that a missed reference would block S3 from compiling.
